None Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree exhibits two fusiform rust cankers located high on the primary stem within the upper canopy. The lower defect is a deeply incised, spiraling fusiform canker partially encircling the stem with pronounced cambial disruption and exposed wood fibers. A second defect is present higher in the scaffold zone, where the canker presents as a fractured lesion within the scaffold branch union area. The defect appears to extend into adjacent limbs.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):30Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:None Reason for Removal:OtherComments:"The terminal leader appears to have been torn out, resulting in an irregular crown structure and redistribution of load within the leader's canopy.
 The canopy exhibits sparse needle density compared to adjacent pines."
		
		
			Tree number:3Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):28Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:None Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree exhibits a spiraling fusiform rust canker high on the primary stem.
